summary: The marginal zone (Zone 2) captures blood-borne antigens from encapsulated bacteria while the periarteriolar lymphoid sheath (Zone 1) activates T and B cells necessary for an effective adaptive immune response—both being crucial for clearing such infections. Red pulp (Zone 3), though vital for filtering damaged erythrocytes, does not participate in producing opsonic antibodies needed to defend against these organisms.
